food_subgroup: Filled and frosted yeast or cake donuts

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general doughnut food product class (FOODON_00002187). No specific filled chocolate pudding donut subclass exists; closest_parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP covers doughnuts as a category. No specific chocolate-filled variant code exists; closest_parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept for chocolate pudding donut identified in the clinical terminology hierarchy.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard chocolate pudding-filled donut weighing approximately 75 g, as typically served at Dunkin' Donuts Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Cake-type doughnut, chocolate-glazed or chocolate-frosted (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, NDB No. 18249)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Values adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for chocolate-glazed cake-type doughnut (NDB 18249 / FDC ID 172757). The Double Choc Pudding Donut is a filled yeast-raised or cake donut with chocolate frosting and chocolate pudding filling sold at Dunkin' Donuts Singapore. No exact Singapore-specific nutritional panel was available from an approved primary source. USDA SR Legacy chocolate-glazed doughnut is the closest defensible analogue. Nutrient values are representative estimates; actual product values may differ slightly due to filling weight and formulation.

gi_source.title: Doughnut, cake-type — GI value from University of Sydney GI Database; analogous estimate applied to chocolate-filled frosted variant
gi_source.source_match_notes: The University of Sydney GI Database reports a GI of approximately 76 for cake-type doughnuts (glucose reference = 100). This value is used as the closest defensible analogous estimate for the Double Choc Pudding Donut, which is a chocolate-frosted, chocolate-pudding-filled donut. The high sugar and refined flour content support a high GI classification. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (76 × 37.5) / 100 ≈ 28.5, using a 75 g serving with 50 g carbohydrate per 100 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content and high refined carbohydrate load
hypertension.guidance: One 75 g serving provides approximately 278 mg sodium (~12% of the 2300 mg daily limit); individuals with hypertension should limit consumption and avoid pairing with other high-sodium foods in the same meal.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (GI 76) and high sugar content (~26 g sugar per 100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: The high GI and glycemic load (~28.5 per serving) of this chocolate pudding donut will cause a rapid postprandial blood glucose spike; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit consumption and monitor blood glucose response carefully.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat, trans fat, and high sugar content from deep-frying and chocolate frosting
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This donut contains saturated fat (~5.2 g/100 g), detectable trans fat (~0.2 g/100 g), and high added sugar, all of which are associated with adverse lipid profiles; individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should treat this as an occasional indulgence only.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Double Chocolate Pudding Donut is an ultra-processed sweet bakery product sold at Dunkin' Donuts outlets in Singapore, including the Funan location. It consists of a deep-fried dough ring or filled donut that is coated with a chocolate glaze or frosting on the exterior and injected with a chocolate pudding cream filling in the interior. The texture is soft and pillowy from the fried dough, with a smooth, sweet, and rich chocolate pudding centre and a thin, slightly firm chocolate glaze on the outside. The flavour profile is intensely sweet and chocolatey, appealing to consumers who enjoy indulgent dessert-style snacks. From a nutritional standpoint, this product is energy-dense at approximately 391 kcal per 100 g, with a high carbohydrate content (~50 g/100 g), significant sugar (~26 g/100 g), and moderate fat (~20 g/100 g) including saturated and trans fats from the frying process and chocolate coating. The glycemic index is high (GI ~76), making it unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or insulin resistance. The sodium content (~370 mg/100 g) is moderate and relevant for hypertension management. This product is not suitable for vegan or gluten-free diets and contains common allergens including gluten, eggs, milk, and soy. It is best treated as an occasional treat rather than a regular dietary item.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a Dunkin Donuts Double Choc Pudding?
Is the Double Choc Pudding donut high in sugar?
What is the GI of a chocolate donut?

Is the Double Choc Pudding donut halal?
Nutrition facts for chocolate filled donut Singapore
How much fat is in a chocolate glazed donut?

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional panel from an approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA Branded Food entry for this specific Dunkin' Donuts Singapore product) was available. Values are adapted from the USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy chocolate-glazed cake-type doughnut entry, which is the closest defensible analogue from an approved secondary global source. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for cake-type doughnuts. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 18249 (chocolate-glazed cake-type doughnut) as the closest approved analogue. Actual product values for the Dunkin' Donuts Singapore Double Choc Pudding Donut may differ due to proprietary formulation, filling weight, and glaze thickness. 2. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry used. 3. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source entry. 4. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source entry. 5. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source entry. 6. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source entry. 7. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source entry. 8. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source entry. 9. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0; confirmed zero — chocolate-glazed donuts contain no meaningful vitamin C per USDA FoodData Central. 10. Halal status is listed as false because Dunkin' Donuts Singapore halal certification status for all outlets and products cannot be confirmed from an approved source at time of processing; this should be verified against MUIS certification records. 11. GI glycemic load per serving calculated using 75 g serving size: (76 × 50.0 × 0.75) / 100 = 28.5. 12. The cuisine field has been updated from the input value 'Coffee' to 'American / International Fast Food' to accurately reflect the product origin and category; the original cuisine_tags (Coffee, Donut, Bagel) reflect the field survey platform category rather than the food's actual cuisine classification.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Initial draft built. Identified that cuisine field 'Coffee' from input was inaccurate; corrected to 'American / International Fast Food'. Added all mandatory multilingual names for Singapore context. Sourced nutrients from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy as closest approved analogue. Added GI from University of Sydney GI Database analogous estimate. Documented all -1 sentinel values in notes. Verified aliases_by_language consistency with flat aliases list. Confirmed vitamin_c_mg = 0.0 with 'confirmed zero' justification in notes.
Iteration 2: Verified GI category threshold: GI 76 ≥ 70 → 'high' confirmed correct. Verified glycemic load calculation. Confirmed all hard gates pass. Confirmed score 8/8. Output finalised as PASS.
